info.title: EFUP: Database migrations deployment + EF Core context/entities generation
info.description: A command-line tool to deploy database migrations and generate EF Core data context/entities 2-in-1 using a single command.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: CommandLineApplication.Parse, OK: CommandLineApplication.Execute, OK: CommandLineApplication.ExecuteAsync, OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole, String), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IHelpTextGenerator, IConsole, String))

options.name: --init
options.description: Use interactive init wizard. You probably want to start with this single option.

options.name: --yes
options.description: Confirm running migrations and generating files for DB context/entities. Without this option EFUP executes in dry run mode.

options.name: --provider
options.description: A database provider. One of `SqlServer` (alias `MSSQL`/`ms`), `PostgreSQL` (`Postgres`/`pg`), `MySQL` (`my`) or `Sqlite` (case insensitive).

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Database connection string to reverse engineer.

options.name: --working-dir
options.description: A working directory override. By default it is the current directory. [default: <path>]

options.name: --data-dir
options.description: A directory inside the working directory where database context and entities are generated. [default: EFUP]

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: A directory inside the working directory with SQL migration files. If this option is missing we use the directory 'DataDir/SQL' but only if it is already present.

options.name: --secrets-key
options.description: A user secret key for a connection string.

options.name: --exclude-default-schema
options.description: Exclude the default schema, e.g. `dbo` for MSSQL or `public` for PG.

options.name: --attributes
options.description: Use EF attribute annotations where possible.

options.name: --schema-prefix
options.description: Add schema name prefix to entity names.

options.name: --strict
options.description: Enable strict mode: no unused configs or unknown schemas/tables.

options.name: --csproj
options.description: Generate a .csproj if it's missing in the working directory.
